chunks.text: In the mid -1990 s, the internet began to enter ordinary homes.
In the thirty years since then, our lives, our work
and the way we connect with one another have all been transformed from the ground up.
The communication that used to be done by letter or by telephone is now carried out instantly through messaging apps and social media.
Even with a friend on the other side of the earth
we can now speak face to face in real time.
The shape of work has also changed.
Through the pandemic, remote work spread rapidly.
Leaving the company office behind, more people now work from home or from a cafe
connected to the world.
But new problems have also been created.
The question of privacy.
The threats to security.
The spread of fake news and misinformation.
Who guarantees the quality of the information we receive, and how?
The algorithm of social media is designed to attract our attention.
Anyone may have noticed, at one moment or another
that they have been staring at their smartphone for hours.
Addiction and digital dependency have become the new illness of modern people.
The internet itself is only a useful tool.
What we use it for, and how we keep our distance from it
depends on the judgment of each one of us.
How to live as a human being inside the digital age — that is the challenge of the generations to come.
